payload.title: Joint Minutes of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority, the New York City Transit Authority, the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority, the Metropolitan Suburban Bus Authority, the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority, the Long Island Rail Road Company, Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company, MTA Construction and Development Company, the MTA Bus Company, Grand Central Madison Operating Company, and the First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company
payload.description: Joint Meeting of the Triborough Bridges & Tunnel Authority, the Long Island Rail Road, Metro-North, Capital Program and New York City Transit, Audit and Finance Committees and the MTA Board — February 25, 2026

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Winter Storm Hernando brought more than two feet of snow and 50mph winds; some areas of Long Island received up to 30 inches with gusts reaching 89 mph. LIRR implemented planned shutdown at 1:00 a.m. Monday.

payload.claim_text: new escalator units were craned through the station roof and lowered into place at Smith-Ninth Street station - the highest elevated subway station in the Western Hemisphere
payload.subject: Smith-Ninth Street station escalator replacement project
payload.location: Smith-Ninth Street station, F and G lines

location_normalized.raw_text: Smith-Ninth Street station, F and G lines

payload.claim_text: the railroad has adopted universal train spotting, a practice commonly used by other commuter railroads. At most stations, all trains will stop at the front of the platform in the direction of travel, regardless of platform length
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad operational change

tool_args.label: Grand Central Terminal event revenue record

payload.claim_text: event revenue reached an all time high of $3.4 million in 2025
payload.subject: Grand Central Terminal event revenue

payload.description: Joint Meeting of the TBTA, LIRR, Metro-North, Capital Program and NYCT, Audit and Finance Committees and the MTA Board

tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 - property acquisition

payload.description: Property acquisition for subsurface easement (19,314 sq. ft.) for tunnel and construction access license (28,442 sq. ft.) for ground improvement work at NYCHA's Senator Robert F. Wagner Senior Houses complex at 2340 Second Avenue. Easement $640,000, license $180,000.

Modified lease terms for 75,000-square-foot bus terminal in Jamaica, Queens. Base rent adjusted to $4 million annually for Years 1-5. 90-year term. Critical to bus operations for MTA Bus, NYCT Bus, and NICE.

Reconstruction of the Grand Central train shed beneath Park Avenue. Private contributions totaling $75 million from JPMorgan Chase, Vornado, Rudin family, and City of New York. Design-build methods used to accelerate delivery.

Combined improvements including connection between 4,5,6 and 7 lines, Times Square Shuttle connection upgrades, access to One Vanderbilt transit hall, circulation links to Grand Central Madison, and new tunnel at 42nd Street and Lexington Avenue. Completed $46 million under budget.

payload.location: Smith-Ninth Street station (F and G lines)
payload.description: Replacing escalators at the highest elevated subway station in the Western Hemisphere. New units craned through station roof.

location_normalized.raw_text: Smith-Ninth Street station (F and G lines)

payload.description: ADA accessibility improvements funded by developer at own expense, including cost overrun responsibility. Discussions ongoing with developers regarding scope, timing, and related LIRR station improvements.

Part of a 13-station accessibility project delivered through a P3 agreement with EAE (JV between Forte and Halmar). Private financing supported construction; developer compensated after demonstrating facilities constructed and maintained to MTA standards.
